title: qPCR based multipathogen detection for Salmonella Paratyphi "A" and Vibrio cholerae from wastewater samples
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7u43u","text":"The following protocol is optimized for the detection of Salmonella Paratyphi A and Vibrio cholerae using multiplex qPCR assays from TNA extracted from environmental samples (wastewater samples). Total Nucleic Acid (TNA) extracted from the pellet particles is used as template in a real-time PCR assay which uses primer-probes specific for Salmonella Paratyphi A or Vibrio cholerae gene targets. Three different multiplex PCR assays were designed following standardization and validation of the primer probe combinations. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":57,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":84,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":340,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":366,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"as45c","text":"This protocol describes qPCR based detection of Salmonella Paratyphi \"A\" and Vibrio cholerae gene targets in three PCR panels from TNA extraction of processed environmental samples that were spiked with internal controls (SPC EGT control DNA - referenced below) . Additionally, HF183 which serves as a marker of fecal contamination in environmental samples, is included in the qPCR assays. Due to the similarity in cycling conditions, these assays can be conducted simultaneously for a sample as three distinct panels.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":48,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":77,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"804hk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fiq8a","text":"The positive controls (PCs) used here are linear oligos (gBlocks) that have been used in generating standard curves. Ideally the concentration of PCs used should yield Ct values that fall in the linear phase of the amplification curve.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c22e7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63fjm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1du53","text":" steps: PCR 2: Vibrio cholerae _1 PCR panel A B C 1 TARGET PRIMERS SEQUENCES 5' TO 3' 2 HF183 Forward ATCATGAGTTCACA GTCCG 3 Reverse CTTCCTCTCAGAACCCCTATCC 4 Probe [FAM] - CTAATGGAACGCATCCC - [BHQ1] 5 wbfO139 Forward AGAAGCCAGTCGCAGTAAAG 6 Reverse TCGCCATCTTCCAGCATAAA 7 Probe [TAMRA] - TGGTGGTACAGCTTAGCCGCATTA - [BHQ2] 8 tcpA classic Forward GCGTAATGCAGCAGCTAATAAA 9 Reverse TATGGGAACATATCACCGACAC 10 Probe [JOE] - ATGGTCTGACACAGGCTCAATGCA - [BHQ1] Table2: Vibrio cholerae _1 PCR Primer and probe sequences. Fluorescent dyes and quenchers are shown in square brackets.

PCR 3: Vibrio cholerae _2 PCR panel A B C 1 TARGET PRIMERS SEQUENCES 5' TO 3' 2 OmpW Forward TCAATGATAGCTGGTTCCTCAAC 3 Reverse CGATGATAAATACCCAAGCATTGA 4 Probe [JOE]_TGGTATGCCAATATTGAAACAACG_[BHQ1] 5 wbeO1 Forward GTTGAGAAGGGCGGTCTAATAA 6 Reverse TGTCTGGTACTTGAGTTGGTAAG 7 Probe [FAM] - TGCCTCAGCAATGGA - [BHQ1] 8 tcpA Eltor Forward ATCCTTTCACTGGTACAGCTATG 9 Reverse GTCAAGCCACCGACTGTAAT 10 Probe [TAMRA]- ACGAAACTCTGCAGCGAATAAAGC-[BHQ2] Table3: Vibrio cholerae _2 PCR Primer and probe sequences. Fluorescent dyes and quenchers are shown in square brackets.

Multiply nmol value by 10 to get the required volume of Nuclease Free Water (NFW) needed to reconstitute the lyophilized primer/probes. Ex: For a primer with 30 nmoles, to make 100 micromolar (µM) stock solution: 30 nmol x 10 = 300 µL of NFW to make 100 micromolar (µM) stock solution.
To reconstitute the lyophilized primers/probes, use the nmole information on the specification sheet received with primers/probes.
Add the required volume of nuclease free water, pulse vortex and spin down. This is the primer/probe stock with 100 micromolar (µM) concentration.
Prepare a working stock from 100 micromolar (µM) stock solution.
Store at -20 °C for long term storage.
Store at 4 °C for frequent usage or -20 °C for long-term storage.
In a fresh tube add 10 µL of 100 micromolar (µM) primer/probe stock and 90 µL of nuclease free water to give 100 µL of 10 micromolar (µM) working primer/probe.

Positive control: gBlocks gene fragments corresponding to each gene target is included in qPCR assay to use as positive control. A B C D 1 gBlock gene Sequence(5'-3') Accession No: bp size 2 SPA2308 ACGATGATGACTGATTTATCGAACAACGACTCTCCCATACAATTTCATTCTTATTGAGAATGCGCTTATGTAATTTATACCCCAGCTCTGAGAGATATCTTTTCA FM200053.1 105 3 ctxA TCCGGAGCATAGAGCTTGGAGGGAAGAGCCGTGGATTCATCATGCACCGCCGGGTTGTGGGAATGCTCCAAGATCATCGA AF463401.1 80 4 HF183 GGGATCATGAGTTCACATGTCCGCATGATTAAAGGTATTTTCCGGTAGACGATGGGGATGCGTTCCATTAGATAGTAGGCGGGGTAACGGCCCACCTAGTCAACGATGGATAGGGGTTCTGAGAGGAAGGTC MT464394.1 132 5 wbfO139 AGAAGCCAGTCGCAGTAAAGCACTAGGGCGCATGGTGGTACAGCTTAGCCGCATTATGCGAGATGAGCCGGGTGCGGATTTTATGCTGGAAGATGGCGA AB012956.1 99 6 tcpA classic GCGTAATGCAGCAGCTAATAAAGCATTTGCAATTTCAGTGGATGGTCTGACACAGGCTCAATGCAAGACACTTATTACCAGTGTCGGTGATATGTTCCCATA M33514.1 102 7 OmpW TCAATGATAGCTGGTTCCTCAACGCTTCTGTGTGGTATGCCAATATTGAAACAACGGCAACCTACAAAGCAGGTGCAGATGCCAAATCCACGGATGTTGAAATCAATCCTTGGGTATTTATGATCG X51948 modified 126 8 wbeO1 GTTGAGAAGGGCGGTCTAATAACACCTAAAGAGTTTGCAGAGAAGCTTGCCTCAGCAATGGATAAGGCTCTTGTACGCTTACCAACTCAAGTACCAGACA KC152957.1 100 9 tcpA El-Tor ATCCTTTCACTGGTACAGCTATGGGGATTTTCTCATTTCCACGAAACTCTGCAGCGAATAAAGCATTCGCAATTACAGTCGGTGGCTTGAC KP187623.1 91 Table4: Sequences used for gBlocks gene fragments
[PCR 1] Salmonella Paratyphi A (SPA2308) and ctxA
The following primers and probes are employed for the detect Salmonella Paratyphi A and Vibrio cholerae specific gene targets.

PCR 1: Salmonella Paratyphi A (SPA2308) and V. cholerae ctxA PCR panel A B C 1 TARGET PRIMERS SEQUENCES 5' TO 3' 2 ctxA Forward TCCGGAGCATAGAGCTTGGA 3 Reverse TCGATGATCTTGGAGCATTCC 4 Probe [CY5] - CCGTGGATTCATCATGCACCGC - [BHQ2] 5 SPA2308 Forward ACGATGATGACTGATTTATCGAAC 6 Reverse TGAAAAGATATCTCTCAGAGCTGG 7 Probe [FAM] - CCCATACAATTTCATTCTTATTGAGAATGCGC - [BHQ1] Table1: SPA and ctxA Primer and probe sequences. Fluorescent dyes and quenchers are shown in square brackets.
Negative control: 3 µL of extraction blank of each batch of extraction. NTC: Master mix alone used for no template control.
Thaw qPCR reagents and samples on ice and briefly spin it down. Prepare master mix for each of the 3 primer-probe panels.
[PCR 3] Vibrio cholerae _2 PCR

Prepare the master mix as follows for the number of samples, positive and negative controls, NTC and one extra reaction to account for any pipetting error. A B 1 REAGENT VOLUME FOR 1 REACTION (μL) 2 UDG Mix 12.5 3 MgCl2 1 4 ROX dye 0.05 5 HF183 F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 6 HF183 R Primer (10 µM) 0.5 7 HF183 Probe (10 µM) 0.25 8 wbfO139 F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 9 wbfO139 R Primer (10 µM) 0.5 10 wbfO139 Probe (10 µM) 0.25 11 tcpA classic F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 12 tcpA classic R Primer (10 µM) 0.5 13 tcpA classic Probe (10 µM) 0.25 14 SPC (10 X EGT Control Mix) 2.5 15 NFW 2.2 Table6: Mastermix composition for Vibrio cholerae _1 PCR gene targets SPC is Sample Processing Control, an optimized TaqMan control designed to be used as qPCR DNA extraction and inhibition control. It is detected by Cy5-labelled probe (Cy5-QXL670 Probe) which comes as ready to use primer probe mix.
[PCR 2] Vibrio cholerae _1 PCR

Prepare the master mix as follows for the number of samples, positive and negative controls, NTC and one extra reaction to account for any pipetting error. A B 1 REAGENT VOLUME FOR 1 REACTION (μL) 2 UDG Mix 12.5 3 MgCl2 1 4 Rox dye 0.05 5 ctxA F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 6 ctxA R Primer (10 µM) 0.5 7 ctxA Probe (10 µM) 0.25 8 SPA2308 F (10 µM) 0.5 9 SPA2308 R (10 µM) 0.5 10 SPA2308 Probe (10 µM) 0.25 11 NFW 5.95 Table5: Mastermix composition for SPA2308 and ctxA gene targets

Prepare the master mix as follows for the number of samples, positive and negative controls, NTC and one extra reaction to account for any pipetting error. A B 1 REAGENT VOLUME FOR 1 REACTION (μL) 2 UDG Mix 12.5 3 MgCl2 1 4 ROX dye 0.05 5 OmpW F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 6 OmpW R Primer (10 µM) 0.5 7 OmpW Probe (10 µM) 0.25 8 wbeO1 F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 9 wbeO1 R Primer (10 µM) 0.5 10 wbeO1 Probe (10 µM) 0.25 11 tcpA El-Tor F Primer (10 µM) 0.5 12 tcpA El-Tor R Primer(10µM) 0.5 13 tcpA El-Tor Probe (10 µM) 0.25 14 NFW 4.7 Table7: Mastermix composition for Vibrio cholerae _2 PCR gene targets
For each PCR, aliquot 22 μl of master mix to each required well in a 96-well plate. Add 3μl of sample, or 3 μl of nuclease free water for negative controls.
Seal the plate with a roller sealer and then centrifuge the plate for 1 min at 2,000g.

Once the run is complete, adjust the thresholds and baseline if any abnormal baseline at the start or at the end is observed, which may lead to a false-positive curve. Verify if the PC is within the range using the cut-off Ct values chosen from running the standards.

Set up the experiment properties with 96-well block, TaqMan reagents, 0.2 ml PCR plate and standard run. Define sample ID and define the targets as described for respective PCR panels. Assign targets and sample ID to each well.
Load the plate into Quantstudio7 flex instrument after proper initiation of the instrument. Open QS7 software, then select "New Experiment set up".
Export the result to excel/csv file and upload both run and csv files.

Set up the PCR cycling method as described below. Cycling conditions remain the same for all 3 qPCRs. A B C 1 Step ( Hold Stage) Temperature Time 2 Reverse Transcription 50°C 2 MIN 3 PCR initial heat activation 95°C 2 MIN 4 2-step cycling (40 cycles) 5 Denaturation 95°C 15 SEC 6 Combined annealing/extension 60°C (data collection step) 30 SEC Start the qPCR by clicking “Run.”

The threshold for each target can be set such that the PC for that target falls within the pre-defined range obtained with the standard curves. The sample is considered positive if the amplification curve is appropriate and the Ct value falls below the defined cut-off thresholds for each target. A separate protocol, provided in the Typhoid ES workspace, serves as an example and can be followed to generate Ct cut-off values: Generating Ct cut-off values using gBlocks gene fragments by Jonathan Rigby
creator: Dilip Abraham
